hero.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Wiki
hero.description: Rhythm Heaven Groove is Nintendo's rhythm game for Nintendo Switch, featuring 80+ single-player rhythm games, 30+ local multiplayer games, Beatspell, a free demo, trailers, and timing tips.

gameFeature.description: Master 80+ solo rhythm games, 30+ local multiplayer challenges, and the unlockable Beatspell rhythm RPG mode on Nintendo Switch.

releaseDateAndPlatforms.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Launch Details
releaseDateAndPlatforms.intro: The fast answer for release date, platform, price, edition, compatibility, publisher, file size, and language searches. Every detail below is confirmed by Nintendo's official product pages.

specs.label: Release Date
specs.detail: Nintendo lists the launch date as July 2, 2026 for Rhythm Heaven Groove.

specs.label: Platform
specs.detail: The game is listed as a Nintendo Switch title on the official store and regional Nintendo pages.

specs.label: Switch 2 Compatibility
specs.detail: Nintendo's U.S. store states that game behavior on Nintendo Switch 2 is consistent with Nintendo Switch.

specs.label: Regular Price
specs.detail: Nintendo's U.S. news page lists the regular price at $39.99.

specs.label: Formats
specs.detail: Nintendo lists a digital eShop version, and regional pages also show a package version with retailer links.

specs.label: Publisher
specs.detail: Nintendo is listed as the publisher on the official product pages.

specs.label: Co-development
specs.detail: Official Nintendo pages credit Nintendo, Tsunku, and co-development by TNX.

specs.label: Players
specs.detail: Single-system multiplayer supports up to four players. Extra compatible controllers may be required.

specs.label: Play Modes
specs.detail: All three Nintendo Switch play modes are listed as supported.

specs.label: File Size
specs.detail: Nintendo's U.S. store lists an estimated 3.5 GB file size for both Nintendo Switch and Nintendo Switch 2.

specs.label: Languages
specs.detail: Japanese, English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Korean, Dutch, and Simplified and Traditional Chinese variants are supported.

specs.label: Rating
specs.detail: The U.S. listing shows an ESRB rating of Mild Fantasy Violence.

demoGuide.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Demo Guide
demoGuide.intro: The free Starter Demo is the cleanest entry point for new players. It teaches the core Rhythm Heaven idea before the full 80-plus solo rhythm game lineup opens up, and it lets returning fans test timing, audio cues, and local multiplayer before buying.

steps.title: Open Nintendo eShop
steps.description: Use Nintendo eShop on Nintendo Switch or Nintendo Switch 2, or open Nintendo's official game page and select the Download demo button.

steps.title: Search the regional title
steps.description: Search for Rhythm Heaven Groove on the U.S. store. On UK and Australia pages, the same game is listed as Rhythm Paradise Groove.

steps.title: Download the free Starter Demo
steps.description: The demo is listed as free and is available from Nintendo eShop and official Nintendo regional pages.

steps.title: Play the first five solo rhythm games
steps.description: Nintendo's UK and Australia pages describe the demo as including the first five solo rhythm games, with Hoop Trundling shown as one confirmed example.

steps.title: Try the multiplayer tweezer challenge
steps.description: The demo also lets players test tweezer timing together with friends, with Rhythm Tweezers highlighted in Nintendo Today coverage.

steps.title: Transfer progress to the full game
steps.description: Progress from the demo can be transferred to the full game, so early practice and cleared demo content continue into the paid version.

steps.title: Use the demo as timing practice
steps.description: The demo is best used to learn the series rhythm rule: listen for the beat and cue first, then press the button at the right moment.

minigamesList.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Minigames List
minigamesList.intro: Nintendo presents Rhythm Heaven Groove as a collection with more than 80 solo rhythm games and more than 30 multiplayer rhythm games. The list below focuses on minigames and named scenarios shown or described by Nintendo and major media coverage.

games.name: Hoop Trundling
games.description: Keep the beat while jumping over hoops. Nintendo's description highlights the cue phrase pa pi pu pe po and jumping on the final beat.

games.name: Hop Stop N Roll
games.description: Control a roly-poly cat doll that jumps and rolls to the music, using simple actions that build timing confidence early.

games.name: Fruit Flex
games.description: Flex your biceps to bop fruit into place, one of the clearest examples of the game's rhythm comedy.

games.name: Slice N Dice Kitchen
games.description: A vegetable-chopping rhythm game shown in official Nintendo materials, matching the broader description of chopping flying veggies.

games.name: Can Do
games.description: A named solo rhythm game shown in Nintendo's official screenshot set for Rhythm Paradise Groove.

games.name: Germ Aerobics
games.description: A named solo rhythm game shown in Nintendo's official screenshot set.

games.name: Soda Hop
games.description: A named solo rhythm game shown in Nintendo's official screenshot set.

games.name: Synchro Wings
games.description: A named solo rhythm game shown in Nintendo's official screenshot set, matching Nintendo's description of flapping along with birds in flight.

games.name: Rhythm Tweezers
games.description: Players take turns plucking a very hairy onion. Nintendo presents it as a cooperative timing challenge where the team must stay sharp.

games.name: Tennis Quest
games.description: A cooperative tennis-themed challenge where players work together to defeat monsters.

games.name: Cake Wait
games.description: Players wait for the exact timing cue to grab a share of cake as the clock strikes 3.

beginnerGuide.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Rhythm Heaven Groove is built around timing, not reaction speed. The game teaches one small action at a time, then asks players to hear the beat, recognize the cue, and press the button on rhythm.

steps.title: Start with the free demo
steps.description: Begin with the Starter Demo to practice the first five solo rhythm games and one multiplayer tweezer challenge before moving into the full game.

steps.title: Watch each minigame intro
steps.description: Rhythm Heaven Groove introduces each rhythm game with a short explanation of the action and the timing window, so learn the rule before chasing the score.

steps.title: Listen before you look
steps.description: Nintendo tells players to use their ears. Some games can trick your eyes, so the beat, cue, and tempo matter more than the animation alone.

steps.title: Practice the early solo games in order
steps.description: Use Hoop Trundling, Hop Stop N Roll, and Fruit Flex as early practice because Nintendo presents them as simple examples of jump, roll, and flex timing.

steps.title: Focus on cue words and tempo changes
steps.description: Listen for each game's beat, button-press action, and tempo cues. Some stages speed up or change the spacing between inputs.

steps.title: Use feedback between attempts
steps.description: Each short rhythm game gives quick performance feedback, making it easy to replay, adjust timing, and build consistency.

steps.title: Progress toward Beatspell
steps.description: Progress in the game to unlock Beatspell, a single-player mode where rhythm-based spell inputs are used to fight monsters.

steps.title: Move into multiplayer after solo practice
steps.description: Once the basic beat feels natural, try the local multiplayer games. Up to four players can play on one system, and each player needs a compatible controller.

quickTips: Listen for the beat first, then watch the screen.
Run TV calibration whenever you change displays or controllers.
Use the demo to learn timing before buying the full game.
Each player needs a compatible controller for four-player local play.

multiplayerModes.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Multiplayer Modes
multiplayerModes.intro: Multiplayer is designed for one Nintendo Switch system and short rhythm challenges with friends. Each player needs a compatible controller, and extra controllers may be required for a full four-player setup.

modes.label: Player Setup
modes.description: Gather up to three friends around one system for single-system multiplayer.
details: TV mode and tabletop mode supported

modes.label: Mode Count
modes.description: The multiplayer set includes both co-operative and competitive rhythm games.
details: Co-op games focus on shared success
Competitive games reward the best timing

modes.label: Co-op Examples
modes.description: Rhythm Tweezers asks players to take turns plucking a hairy onion, while Tennis Quest turns rhythm timing into a shared monster-fighting challenge.

modes.label: Competitive Examples
modes.value: Beat your friends to the cue
modes.description: Cake Wait has players grab cake right as the clock hits 3, and other shown scenarios include racing wrestlers, arrow slicing, and rolling ball survival challenges.

beatspellMode.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Beatspell Mode
beatspellMode.intro: Beatspell sits outside the standard rhythm-game list and gives Rhythm Heaven Groove a story-focused battle mode. Progress through the main game to unlock it, then earn medals in solo rhythm games to open new chapters.

features.label: Unlock Path
features.description: Beatspell becomes available after progressing in the main game, and new chapters unlock as medals are earned in solo rhythm games.

features.label: Battle Loop
features.description: Players tap buttons to the correct beat to cast rhythm-based spells during monster encounters.

features.label: Spell Roles
features.description: Shown spell actions include hurling fire at enemies and healing yourself, with different rhythms used for different spell effects.

features.label: Progression
features.description: Beatspell includes ability growth and story chapters, giving players a longer-form mode beyond standard rhythm-game clears.

features.label: Mode Difference
features.description: Main rhythm games are short, self-contained timing tests; Beatspell uses rhythm skill inside battles, spells, enemies, and chapter progression.

controlsAndAccessibility.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Controls and Accessibility
controlsAndAccessibility.intro: Most games ask for button presses on the beat while the music, cue sounds, and tempo changes guide the timing. The game supports TV, tabletop, and handheld play, with accessibility options for text reading and timing calibration.

faqs.question: How do the basic controls work in Rhythm Heaven Groove?
faqs.answer: The core rule is to press buttons in time with the beat. Many rhythm games use a small number of button inputs, then change the cue pattern, tempo, or visual distraction to test listening and timing.

faqs.question: Should Rhythm Heaven Groove players watch the screen or listen to the music?
faqs.answer: Listening is the safer habit. Some games can trick your eyes, so players should follow the beat, button-action cues, and tempo instead of relying only on visual prompts.

faqs.question: What does TV calibration do in Rhythm Heaven Groove?
faqs.answer: The game includes calibration options to fine-tune button timing for TV play. Run calibration when switching displays, changing controllers, or noticing that inputs feel slightly late or early.

faqs.question: What accessibility setting does Rhythm Heaven Groove list for text?
faqs.answer: On-screen text can be read out loud by the in-game assistant Li'l Miss Reeds, helping players who prefer spoken guidance.

faqs.question: Which play modes does Rhythm Heaven Groove support?
faqs.answer: TV mode, tabletop mode, and handheld mode are supported. Nintendo notes that Nintendo Switch Lite play experience may differ, and multiplayer can require additional controllers.

faqs.question: What should Rhythm Heaven Groove players check before multiplayer?
faqs.answer: Use one compatible controller per player. For four-player local play, prepare enough controllers before starting the multiplayer set.

trailersAndVideos.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove Trailers and Gameplay Videos
trailersAndVideos.intro: The video set shows how Rhythm Heaven Groove presents its rhythm games, party play, and side modes. Start with the reveal trailer for the original announcement, then watch the overview trailer and Treehouse footage for multiplayer, Beatspell, and hands-on gameplay flow.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Rhythm Heaven Groove

questions.question: When does Rhythm Heaven Groove release and on which platforms?
questions.answer: Rhythm Heaven Groove launches on Nintendo Switch on July 2, 2026, and Nintendo's U.S. store confirms Nintendo Switch 2 compatibility is supported.

questions.question: Is there a free demo for Rhythm Heaven Groove?
questions.answer: Yes. The free Starter Demo includes the first five solo rhythm games and a multiplayer tweezer challenge, and demo progress can be transferred to the full game.

questions.question: How many minigames does Rhythm Heaven Groove have?
questions.answer: Rhythm Heaven Groove includes more than 80 single-player rhythm games and more than 30 local multiplayer rhythm games, plus the unlockable Beatspell mode.

questions.question: Does Rhythm Heaven Groove support multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Rhythm Heaven Groove supports 1-4 players on a single Nintendo Switch system for local co-op and competitive play. Online multiplayer is not supported.

questions.question: What is Beatspell in Rhythm Heaven Groove?
questions.answer: Beatspell is an unlockable single-player rhythm RPG mode in Rhythm Heaven Groove where button timing becomes spellcasting, healing, and monster battles.

questions.question: How much does Rhythm Heaven Groove cost?
questions.answer: Rhythm Heaven Groove has a regular price of $39.99 on the Nintendo Switch eShop, with both digital and package editions available depending on region.
